checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: DSO rose from ~76 days (2010) to ~90 days (2012) using AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=92042243.85 (2012-12-31) vs SalesRevenueServicesNet=371234727.66 (2012 annual), before improving to ~84 days by FY2013 (AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=103968885.07 vs SalesRevenueServicesNet=453889907.19)
evidence.location: 10-K FY2013 balance sheet and income statement

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueServicesNet grew only ~126% over the same period (164517492.11 in 2010 to 371234727.66 in 2012)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No InventoryNet concept was provided in the dataset, consistent with a services business (SalesRevenueServicesNet, CostOfServices)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Quarterly NetIncomeLoss for 2013: 2702955.55 (Q1), 1321444.94 (Q2), 3736085.23 (Q3), 4161350.24 (Q4) shows normal quarter-to-quarter volatility, not artificial smoothness

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=588643.65 (2010-12-31) dropped to 245067.97 (2011-12-31) and remained flat at 245067.97 through 2012-09-30, while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 34345555.24 (2010-12-31) to 79822482.12 (2012-09-30)

Allowance as % of gross receivables fell from ~1.72% (2010-12-31: 588643.65/34345555.24) to ~0.40% (2011-12-31: 245067.97/60812896.03) and remained below 1% through 2013-12-31 (679943.49/103968885.07=0.65%) despite AR nearly tripling

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 8-K/A filings on 2012-03-05 and 2012-10-24, consistent with pro forma financial statement amendments typical of active acquirers

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Data spans annual and quarterly concepts from FY2009 through FY2013 (e.g., SalesRevenueServicesNet from 2009-01-01/2009-12-31 through 2013-01-01/2013-12-31) across multiple 10-K and 10-Q filings

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential under-provisioning for doubtful accounts as receivables grew rapidly post-IPO and via acquisitions; the allowance-to-receivable ratio dropped sharply (from ~1.7% to ~0.4%) and stayed below historical levels even as gross receivables nearly tripled, which could understate bad debt expense and overstate net receivables and reported net income.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=588643.65 (2010-12-31) vs 245067.97 (2011-12-31) while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 34345555.24 to 60812896.03 over the same period
AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=679943.49 (2013-12-31) vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=103968885.07 (2013-12-31), a ratio of only 0.65%

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Receivables grew materially faster than revenue in FY2011 (77% vs 53%), extending days-sales-outstanding from ~76 to ~90 days by FY2012, which could indicate aggressive revenue recognition, extended customer payment terms, or channel-stuffing type behavior around the IPO period; this reversed somewhat by FY2013.

Rapid, acquisition-driven build-up of goodwill and intangible assets (goodwill nearly quadrupled 2010-2012; intangibles nearly tripled) without any observable impairment charge in the data provided raises risk that carrying values may not fully reflect the fair value of acquired businesses if post-acquisition performance falls short, though this is explainable by legitimate M&A activity evident in the filing chronology (8-K/A amendments).

top_signals: Allowance for doubtful accounts as % of gross receivables dropped sharply from ~1.7% (2010) to ~0.4% (2011) and remained below 1% through 2013 despite AR nearly tripling (34.3M to 104.0M), a classic under-reserving signal
Receivables grew faster than revenue in FY2011 (77% vs 53% growth), extending DSO from ~76 to ~90 days before partially normalizing by FY2013

Goodwill and intangible assets grew far faster than revenue in 2011-2012 due to acquisitions, elevating 'soft asset' concentration on the balance sheet with no observable impairment testing detail provided

Positive offsetting factor: operating cash flow consistently and substantially exceeded net income in all profitable years (e.g., OCF=51.4M vs NI=11.9M in FY2013), which is inconsistent with classic earnings-inflation manipulation and instead suggests GAAP income is being suppressed by non-cash acquisition-related amortization

No late filings (NT forms) or restatements detected in filing chronology; amendments (10-K/A, 8-K/A) appear consistent with routine M&A disclosure requirements rather than financial statement corrections
